data.title: Bitcoin marks 8th anniversary of Block Size Wars with significant value growth post-independence from miner control on Aug. 1, 2017.
data.description: Since August 1, 2017, the value of Bitcoin has significantly increased following the Bitcoin community's move to break free from miner control.

data.title: Canaan Inc. adopts Bitcoin (BTC) as primary reserve asset in new policy, focusing on growth and liquidity.
data.description: Canaan Inc. recently announced its new cryptocurrency holding policy, highlighting Bitcoin as the primary reserve asset to meet strategic growth and liquidity requirements. This move comes as part of the company's strategy to secure its financial position in the volatile crypto market. Canaan Inc. aims to maintain a substantial amount of Bitcoin in its reserves to support its long-term objectives. The decision signifies a shift towards a more Bitcoin-centric approach within the company. This policy update reflects Canaan Inc.'s commitment to adapting to the evolving crypto landscape by ensuring a significant portion of its assets – a noteworthy development in the industry.

data.title: New tool Gemini considers + executes crypto trades. Examined for effectiveness, it aids in some aspects but not all.

The review delves into the platform's strengths and weaknesses, highlighting areas where it excels and where it falls short.

data.title: NextNav and Oscilloquartz team up to improve 5G timing solutions for critical infrastructure, aiming to provide alternative to GPS for systems such as data centers, utilities and emergency services.
data.description: NextNav, a top player in resilient Positioning, Navigation, and Timing (PNT) technologies, revealed a significant partnership with Oscilloquartz, a prominent global leader in time synchronization solutions. This collaboration aims to highlight the potential of 5G-powered terrestrial PNT and Oscilloquartz’s established timing technology. Together, they plan to offer a reliable complement and backup to GPS, ensuring the continuity of essential systems such as data centers, utilities, and emergency services.

data.title: Fed doesn't cut rates despite Trump pressure, reveals internal divide. Interest rates remain at 4.25%-4.50%. Two Governors dissent for first time since 1993.
data.description: Amid mounting expectations for a pivotal Federal Reserve meeting, the central bank's decision to maintain interest rates at 4.25%-4.50% in July did not come as a surprise to the market. However, the meeting revealed a significant internal discord within the Federal Open Market Committee. This marked the first time since 1993 that two permanent members of the Fed's Board of Governors, Michelle Bowman and Christopher Waller, openly dissented, advocating for an immediate 25 basis-point rate reduction. This level of dissent among members has not been witnessed since the era of Alan Greenspan. The development highlights a rare split within the Fed, shedding light on the notable internal tensions. More information is available on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Udemy CEO to speak at Canaccord Genuity 45th Annual Growth Conference on Aug 12, 2025, 9:00 a.m. ET. Visit investor relations website for live webcast.
data.description: In San Francisco, Udemy, a prominent AI-driven skills acceleration platform listed on Nasdaq as UDMY, revealed that CEO Hugo Sarrazin will engage in a fireside chat session at the Canaccord Genuity 45th Annual Growth Conference. The session is scheduled for Tuesday, August 12, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. ET. Interested individuals can access a live webcast of the discussion via Udemy's investor relations website at https://investors.udemy.com/.
Brent Crude futures settle at $69.67/bbl, down $2.03, 2.83%.

data.title: NIIMBL launches Project Call 9.1, offering $8 million for biopharma innovation projects in technology and workforce development.
data.description: In Newark, Delaware, on August 1, 2025, the National Institute for Innovation in Manufacturing Biopharmaceuticals (NIIMBL) has revealed details about Project Call 9.1 aimed at promoting technology and workforce development initiatives. NIIMBL has set aside a substantial funding of $8 million to support new project activities in this latest call.

data.title: Trump wants BLS chief fired over disappointing jobs report: 73,000 jobs in July, significant cuts to May and June numbers.
data.description: In July, the monthly jobs report revealed a total of only 73,000 new jobs, reflecting significant decreases in the previously reported numbers for both May and June.

data.title: Corp for Public Broadcasting shutting down due to loss of federal funding, rejected their plea for increase to $834 million.
data.description: On August 1, 2025, National Press Club President Mike Balsamo responded to the Corporation for Public Broadcasting's declaration of ceasing operations due to the termination of federal funding. This decision comes as a significant blow, impacting [insert specific number] employees and [insert specific number] programs that rely on this support. Balsamo emphasized the vital role public broadcasting plays in providing diverse and informative content to audiences across the nation. The effects of this funding cut will undoubtedly have far-reaching consequences on the media landscape and the public's access to unbiased information.

data.title: 85-year-old Ross Rowland, a railroad preservationist with Wall St. background, passed. He funded American Freedom Train and other historic trips.
data.description: After amassing a sizable wealth as a commodities broker, he invested in the American Freedom Train and other notable vintage trips.

data.title: Public Broadcasting Corporation to close due to $500 million cut in federal funds for NPR, PBS, and local stations.
data.description: The corporation is one of the initial victims of a decision to remove about $500 million in federal funding from NPR, PBS, and regional stations nationwide.

data.title: Avantor Inc. (NYSE: AVTR) Q2 reports lower sales impacted by bioprocessing shortcomings. adjusted EPS of 24 cents, sales of $1.68B, down 1% from 2024. Share price near 52-week low.
data.description: Avantor Inc. (NYSE: AVTR) saw a decline in its stock on Friday following the release of its second-quarter 2025 financial results. The company reported an adjusted EPS of 24 cents, slightly below the expected 25 cents. With sales totaling $1.68 billion, in line with the $1.675 billion consensus, AVTR experienced a 1% decrease compared to the same period in 2024. Foreign currency translation had a 2% positive impact, while M&A had a negative 3% impact, resulting in flat organic sales. Laboratory Solutions sales were reported at $1.12 billion, a 3% decrease with a 1% organic sales drop. Bioscience Production sales amounted to $561.3 million, also down 3%, but up 2% organically. The company noted that bioprocessing performance did not meet expectations. AVTR shares are close to their 52-week low. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

data.title: Cryptocurrency market down with Bitcoin at $113,782.50, Ethereum at $3,547.80, XRP at $2.99, Dogecoin at $0.2049. Liquidations top $821.71 million. Shiba Inu burns at 5,800%. Trump calls for Powell removal. Coinbase not seen as Visa, Mastercard competition.
data.description: Cryptocurrency markets are experiencing a decline on Friday, mirroring the drop in equity markets. As of now, Bitcoin (BTC) is priced at $113,782.50, Ethereum (ETH) at $3,547.80, Solana (SOL) at $166.20, XRP at $2.99, Dogecoin (DOGE) at $0.2049, and Shiba Inu (SHIB) at $0.00001209. Notably, data from IntoTheBlock reveals that large transactions in Bitcoin have decreased by 8.6% while Ethereum saw an increase of 6.5%. Additionally, daily active addresses surged by 5.3% for Bitcoin but slightly decreased by 0.1% for Ethereum. Coinglass data indicates that 189,265 traders were liquidated in the last 24 hours, resulting in losses of $821.71 million. The biggest losers include Hyperliquid (HYPE), Pump.fun (PUMP), and SPX6900 (SPX). In other news, Shiba Inu is marking its 5th anniversary with a burn rate spike of 5,800%. There is also a report on Trump urging the Fed Board to replace Chair Jerome Powell, despite skepticism from Polymarket traders. Coinbase's CEO Brian Armstrong asserts that the platform is not in direct competition with Visa or Mastercard in the realm of crypto payments. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.
